data.title: Byrna Technologies rises after reporting Q3 results
data.description:
• Byrna Technologies press release (NASDAQ:BYRN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.09 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $28.18M (+35.2% Y/Y) in-line.
  • The year-over-year growth was largely driven by strong dealer and chain store sales tied to Byrna’s expanding retail presence, the success of new marketing initiatives, and broader brand adoption. Web traffic began to build late in the quarter and has continued into the fourth fiscal quarter of 2025 (Q4 2025), supporting stronger e-commerce activity.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: TLRY, AKRO, DAL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched higher in premarket trading on Thursday, following a strong tech rally that pushed the Nasdaq to a new record high in the previous session.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Richardson Electronics (NASDAQ:RELL) +22% - Shares jumped higher after the company reported better-than-expected FQ1 results, driven by strong growth in its semiconductor wafer fab business. Excluding the divested Healthcare unit, net sales rose by 6.8% Y/Y. The company's focus on a more profitable sales mix and disciplined cost control was highly effective, leading its operating income to more than triple compared to the prior year. CEO Edward J. Richardson noted the solid results reflect the company's value proposition and market diversity, adding that they generated positive operating cash flow for the sixth consecutive quarter and are confident in delivering improved performance for the entirety of FY2026.
  • Akero Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AKRO) +19% - Shares advanced after the company announced a definitive agreement to be acquired by Novo Nordisk (NVO) for up to $5.2B in cash. Under the agreement, Akero shareholders will get $54.00 in cash for each share when the deal closes, plus a $6.00 payment that will be made if efruxifermin gets full U.S. regulatory approval for MASH-related compensated cirrhosis The upfront cash portion values Akero at $4.7B, representing a 42% premium to its May 19, 2025, closing price. The deal, unanimously approved by Akero’s board, is expected to close by year-end.
  • Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) +13% - Shares surged after the Canadian cannabis company posted its first revenue beat in four quarters and raised its full-year outlook. Q1 revenue rose ~5% Y/Y to $209.5M, topping estimates by ~$3.8M, driven by a 9% rise in distribution sales to $74M, while cannabis revenue grew ~5% to $64.5M and beverages were flat at $55.7M. Operating costs fell ~43% to $55.4M amid lower amortization and non-cash expenses, helping Tilray swing to $1.5M net income from a $34.7M loss a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA rose ~9% to $10.2M, and adjusted net income improved to $3.9M from a $6.1M loss, though gross margin slipped to 27% from 30%. The company now expects FY2026 adjusted EBITDA of $62M–$72M, above analysts’ $61.1M forecast.
  • Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) +5% - Shares rose after the carrier delivered a stronger-than-expected Q3, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.71, beating estimates by $0.19, and adjusted revenue of $15.2B, up 4.2% Y/Y and $120M above consensus. For FY2025, Delta guided EPS of around $6, exceeding the $5.77 consensus, with free cash flow of $3.5B–$4B and gross leverage below 2.5x. In Q4, the airline expects revenue growth of 2%–4% vs. the 1.24% forecast, an operating margin of 10.5%–12%, and EPS of $1.60–$1.90, with a midpoint of $1.75, above the $1.64 consensus.

Biggest stock losers

  • Apogee Therapeutics (NASDAQ:APGE) -10% - Shares plunged after the company priced its public offering at $41 per share, raising approximately $300M in gross proceeds. The offering comprises 6.95M common shares and 365,853 pre-funded warrants, both priced at $41, with the warrants exercisable immediately. The deal, expected to close on October 10, 2025, also grants underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 1.1M additional shares. Apogee said proceeds will support advancement of its clinical-stage biologics pipeline targeting inflammatory and immunology markets.
  • AZZ (NYSE:AZZ) -6% - Shares slid after the company missed FQ2 expectations and presented a mixed outlook for the full year. While AZZ maintained its FY2026 guidance, the midpoint for its projected sales of $1.675B came in slightly above the consensus of $1.66B. However, the midpoint for adjusted diluted EPS of $6.00 missed the analyst consensus of $6.04. The maintained guidance reflects expectations for anticipated market conditions, lower interest expense, and a 24% effective tax rate, while excluding any M&A activity or federal regulatory changes.

data.title: Zefiro investigating former CEO
data.description:
• Zefiro (NEOE:ZEFI:CA) said on Thursday that it has commenced an internal investigation to assess whether any potential misconduct may have occurred involving director Talal Debs. 
  • This internal investigation follows the filing of a civil lawsuit in which Debs is named as a defendant. The complaint includes allegations concerning his conduct both before and during his tenure as chief executive officer of the company. 
  • Debs served as CEO from December 1, 2023, until his removal on June 6, 2025, and remains a member of the company's board. 
  • The board recently became aware of a complaint filed in New York against Debs concerning his alleged misconduct at X Machina Sustainable Technologies ("XMST") and its related entities, including X Machina Capital Strategies (XMC). 
  • While XMST and XMC continue to be significant shareholders of the company, Debs's role and control of XMST are subject to that dispute. 
  • During a board meeting on October 7, 2025, the board determined that an investigation was necessary because of alleged improprieties in the complaint against Debs in handling funds and other alleged misconduct in his capacity as a fiduciary.

data.title: InMode’s Q3 revenue seen above estimates
data.description:
• InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) said it expects third-quarter 2025 revenue to come in between $92.5 million and $93 million (consensus: $88.05M), according to preliminary results released Thursday.
  • The medical technology company, which is still finalizing its full financial report, projected non-GAAP gross margins between 77% and 79% for the period.
  • For the full year 2025, InMode reaffirmed its revenue outlook of $365 million to $375 million (consensus: $368.45M), in line with its previous guidance.
  • The company will release its financial results for the third quarter before the Nasdaq market opens on November 5, 2025.
  • INMD +2.9% premarket to $15.50.

data.title: Tilray Brands in charts: FQ1 Cannabis revenue up 5% Y/Y, while Alcohol and Wellness segments see sharp Q/Q declines
INEO Tech announces $0.5M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• INEO Tech (INEO:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $0.5M offering, under which the company will issue 12.5M shares at a price of $0.04 per share.
  • The net proceeds will be used to fund the company's growth plans by covering working capital and general corporate purposes, including inventory & deployments and sales & marketing initiatives.

data.title: ARKO names Jordan Mann as interim CFO
data.description:
• ARKO (NASDAQ:ARKO) announced that Jordan Mann, the company’s senior vice president of corporate strategy, capital markets, and investor relations, has been appointed interim chief financial officer, effective October 10, 2025.
  • Mann brings more than a decade of experience in finance and capital markets to the role. Before joining ARKO in 2023, he held senior positions in investment banking at Morgan Stanley, where he served as an Executive Director, and at Credit Suisse, where he was a Director.
  • He succeeds Robb Giammatteo, whose departure was previously announced.

data.title: Polestar Q3 2025 retail sales rise 13%, year-to-date deliveries Up 36%
data.description:
• Polestar (NASDAQ:PSNY) reported Q3 2025 retail sales of 14,192 vehicles, up 13% from 12,548 in Q3 2024.
  • Year-to-date (Q1–Q3 2025), sales reached 44,482 cars, marking a 36% increase over the same period in 2024 (32,595 cars).
  • PSNY has already matched full-year 2024 sales volume in just the first nine months of 2025.
  • PSNY cited strong order intake and the current EV lineup as key growth drivers, despite challenging market conditions.
  • Polestar will release select Q3 2025 financial results on November 12, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BeMetals raises $1.1M through the sale of marketable securities
data.description:
• BeMetals (TSXV:BMET:CA) said on Thursday that it has raised proceeds of approximately C$1.13M through the sales of marketable securities held by the company. 
  • The sales were conducted through the facilities of the TSX Venture Exchange. The proceeds from the sales strengthen BeMetals' cash position and bring its current cash balance to a total of approximately C$1.52M. 
  • BeMetals also announces it intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of up to 10.7M common shares of the company that qualify as "flow-through shares", at a price of $0.07 per FT share for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$749,000.
  • The company intends to use the gross proceeds of the private placement to incur qualifying expenditures on the company's Savant Gold Project, located in Ontario, Canada.

data.title: Meet Elettrica: Ferrari's answer to EVs featuring 1000 horsepower and a top speed of 192 mph
data.description:
Ferrari N.V. (NYSE:RACE) unveiled some details about its first electric vehicle, which has a placeholder name of Elettrica. The EV features in-house-developed electric motors, producing over 1,000 horsepower and enabling a 0-62 mph acceleration speed of 2.5 seconds. The Elettrica will have a range of over 323 miles and a top speed of 192 mph, powered by a 122 kWh battery pack.

The Elettrica will be primarily all-wheel drive, although the front motors can be switched off to create rear-wheel drive by selecting the proper e-Manettino mode.

The automaker said a unique engineered sound system that uses real powertrain vibrations will create a novel, emotive electric Ferrari sound, which will set it apart from other electric vehicles. The initial skateboard platform indicates a four-door configuration that seats four or more passengers. However, for now, it is unclear if the EV will be a sedan or SUV. Ferrari (NYSE:RACE) did not provide any concept pictures of the model.

Ferrari (RACE) said the unveiling of the new Elettrica will continue in early 2026 with a preview of the look and feel of the interior design concepts. A few months later, in spring next year, the company will host a world premiere where the technology and design will be revealed.

As part of its Capital Markets Day presentation, Ferrari (RACE) lowered its forecast for electric vehicles to account for 20% of its lineup by 2030, down from a prior target of 40%.

Shares of Ferrari (RACE) were down 13.3% in premarket trading following the long-term guidance update earlier in the day.
